publicationStmt.p: Transcribed from Bodleian Library, Lovelace Byron collection, Box 165, folios 178-183. Transcription by Lisa Gold (2022). TEI conversion for Sextant pipeline, 2026.

div.p: Saturday
Dearest W. I have given your letters to the Hen, who has been with me this afternoon.
From the enclosed (received from her on Thursday), you will perceive that I had stated to you quite correctly what she told me about Lady
H, but that her opinion subsequently altered, on re-consideration.
I am in great delight & excitement, at finding I am to learn singing of Herr Staudigl, when he arrives for the German Opera. There is no such singer & dramatist I believe in Europe.
Mr Rousselet has decided this choice of a master for me.-- I had left it to Rousselet, knowing that
he is the best of judges on such a subject.--

Rousselet is astonished at my powers, both musical & dramatic, & says that I must sing Scenas by & bye; as such a genius must not be lost to my friends & society. In scena - singing you know there is real acting , just as on the stage. For example I should sing a scena

from Norma, (one of those between her & Pollio say), in the little-library at Ockham, I & my Pollio being in our ordinary dresses, but doing it exactly as is done on the stage; & the audience sitting in the large library, we merely placing ourselves so as to be seen thro' the folding-doors.--
This would be to me the greatest of enjoyments,

& will give you much to be proud & fond of. They all say that my dramatic genius is something wonderful; & the more scope I have in prospect for it, the more settled, calm, & happy, does my mind become. The style of song that best suits me is that in which there is the expression of deep & stirring & generous sentiments, like those
in Norma for instance; & also those in which there is vengeance [very thick underline] scorn & indignation. Perhaps the latter you would not have imagined would be my line.
But it is marvellous, they tell me, how powerfully I can express scorn & fury, & get my action being all the while so tranquil & so removed from the

stage . But it is with a glance, on the motion of an arm or hand, that I [two words crossed out] so naturally & so powerfully give those feelings. There is a famous song of Norma's in which she says the single words "Indegno," (to Pollio), "tu preghi alfine."
The manner in which I do this is I believe admirable, in its mixture of scornful pride and indignation. I don't think
I am growing conceited, pray. But I speak of myself as I should of another; & rejoice in the pleasure I think my rare musical genius will by & bye be able to afford to you, & others.

A singing & acting Avis, would be delightful. Think how mwerrily & joyously evenings would go; & how delightful when we have company, to be able to inspire them a little, as I know I could, thro' song!

It is a great & glorious gift to have, where as with me it results from deep & powerful & generous feelings. For there is a mysterious kind of mesmerism in such expression as I am likely to be able to give, which ennobles the hearts of those who listen .--
May I be one day worthy of the name of Phoenix, which you now bestow on
one who as yet has done little to deserve it.--
